evals.name: Basic Live Script Structure
evals.description: Tests that a simple tutorial Live Script has correct structure: sections, text lines, appendix, no banned patterns
evals.prompt: Create a Live Script called BasicSignals.m that introduces the reader to generating sine and cosine waves in MATLAB. Include a title, a brief intro paragraph, two sections (one per wave type) each with a plot, and a short summary section. Save the file to the current directory.

assertions.description: Live Script file was created
Uses %[text] for text lines
Section breaks include the %[text] ## heading follow-up
assertions.expected: Every %% line on its own is followed within 1-2 lines by a %[text] ## heading line. PASS: %% appears alone on its line, then within 2 lines a %[text] ## Title line appears (intervening blank lines are not allowed by the skill, but code lines between are acceptable). FAIL: a %% break with no %[text] ## heading nearby, or %% with prose only and no markdown heading.

assertions.description: Has required appendix
No figure command in the generated .m file
assertions.expected: The generated .m file does not contain a standalone 'figure' or 'figure(' call as a MATLAB command. Ignore any mention of the word 'figure' in %[text] prose lines.

assertions.description: No close all in the generated .m file
assertions.expected: The generated .m file does not contain 'close all' as a MATLAB command on any code line (lines that don't start with %).

assertions.description: No clear command in the generated .m file
assertions.expected: The generated .m file does not contain 'clear' as a standalone MATLAB command on any code line (lines that don't start with %). The word 'clear' appearing in %[text] prose is acceptable.

assertions.description: The generated Live Script runs without error

evals.name: Escaping Special Characters
evals.description: Tests that the skill correctly escapes underscores, dots after digits, and other special characters in text lines
evals.prompt: Create a Live Script called EscapingDemo.m that explains MATLAB variable naming rules. In the text, mention variable names like my_variable, sample_rate, and x_0. Also mention that MATLAB version 9.14 introduced new features. Include a table showing 3 variable naming conventions with examples. Save to current directory.

assertions.description: Live Script file was created
Underscores are escaped in text lines
Some attempt at escaping dots after digits in %[text] lines
assertions.expected: Escaping the period after a digit is a defensive precaution, not strictly required for rendering. PASS: at least one occurrence of a digit-dot sequence in %[text] lines is escaped as \. (e.g., 9\.14), OR the prose simply does not include any digit-dot sequences. FAIL only if the file contains multiple unambiguous digit-dot sequences in %[text] prose AND none of them are escaped.

assertions.description: Has a table block
Some attempt at escaping inside table cells
assertions.expected: Escaping inside %[text:table] cells is a defensive precaution. PASS: underscores or dots-after-digits inside the table show at least some escaping (\_ or \.) consistent with how they are escaped in surrounding %[text] lines, OR the table cells contain no characters that need escaping. FAIL only if the table is full of underscores/digit-dots that are clearly unescaped while the surrounding prose escapes them.

evals.name: LaTeX Equations
evals.description: Tests correct LaTeX rendering with proper escaping in equations
evals.prompt: Create a Live Script called FourierIntro.m that explains the Discrete Fourier Transform. Include the DFT formula as a display equation in the text, mention that the transform maps N samples to N frequency bins, and show a simple example computing the FFT of a 1024-point signal. Save to current directory.

assertions.description: Live Script file was created
Contains LaTeX equation delimiters
LaTeX uses double backslashes for commands
assertions.expected: LaTeX commands like \sum, \pi, \frac use double backslashes (e.g., \\sum, \\pi, \\frac)

assertions.description: LaTeX subscripts do not use the broken double-backslash form
assertions.expected: Bare _ in LaTeX renders correctly on first save, and \_ is the documented preferred form — both are acceptable. The only hard failure is \\_ (double backslash + underscore) which produces a literal backslash and breaks the equation. PASS: any subscripts use either bare _ or \_. FAIL: any LaTeX subscript uses \\_.

assertions.description: The generated code runs without error

evals.name: Lists and Formatting
evals.description: Tests bullet lists, numbered lists, bold, italic, and the trailing backslash rule
evals.prompt: Create a Live Script called WorkflowGuide.m that describes a 5-step data analysis workflow. Use a numbered list for the steps, include bold text for step names, italic for optional notes, and a bulleted list of 3 tips at the end. Save to current directory.

assertions.description: Live Script file was created
Uses numbered list syntax
Uses bullet list syntax
Last item of each list ends with backslash
assertions.expected: The final item in every bulleted list and every numbered list ends with a trailing backslash (\) before the next non-list line

assertions.description: Uses bold formatting
No blank lines except optionally one before the appendix
assertions.expected: The file contains no blank lines, except for at most a single blank line directly before the %[appendix]{"version":"1.0"} marker. No empty lines anywhere else in the file.

evals.name: No Banned Patterns
evals.description: Tests that the skill avoids mfilename, fprintf, and bare % comments
evals.prompt: Create a Live Script called DataLoader.m that loads a CSV file, displays summary statistics, and plots the first column. The script should print the filename being loaded and show the number of rows. Save to current directory.

assertions.description: Live Script file was created
No mfilename usage in the generated .m file
assertions.expected: The generated .m file does not use 'mfilename' as a MATLAB function call on any code line. The skill explicitly forbids it because it returns empty in Live Scripts.

assertions.description: No fprintf; uses disp() or drop-semicolon for output
assertions.expected: The generated .m file does not use 'fprintf' on any code line, AND uses one of the documented alternatives for reader-visible output: either at least one disp() call, or at least one expression statement that intentionally drops the trailing semicolon to display its value. PASS: fprintf absent AND (disp present OR a non-semicolon-terminated output expression present). FAIL: fprintf appears on any code line, OR fprintf is absent but the script uses neither disp() nor drop-semicolon for reader output.

assertions.description: All text lines use %[text] not bare % comments
assertions.expected: Every line of prose/explanatory text uses %[text] syntax, not bare % comment syntax. Code comments (% after code on the same line) are acceptable.

assertions.description: Has the required appendix

evals.name: Anchors and Internal Links
evals.description: Tests that the skill correctly uses anchors and internal hyperlinks to navigate within a Live Script
evals.prompt: Create a Live Script called NavDemo.m with three sections (Introduction, Method, Results). At the top, include a brief navigation paragraph with internal links that jump to the Method and Results sections. Add anchors on those section headings so the links work. Save to current directory.

assertions.description: Live Script file was created
Uses anchor syntax on a heading
Uses internal link syntax
Anchor IDs use only letters, digits, and underscores (no hyphens)
assertions.expected: Every %[text:anchor:ID] in the file uses an ID containing only alphanumeric characters and underscores. No hyphens appear in any anchor ID, because hyphens prevent the anchor from binding.

assertions.description: Internal link IDs match defined anchor IDs
assertions.expected: Every (internal:ID) reference in the file targets an ID that is defined by a corresponding %[text:anchor:ID] elsewhere in the file.

evals.name: No Hard-Wrapping in Prose Paragraphs
evals.description: Tests that multi-sentence prose paragraphs are written on a single %[text] line rather than split across multiple %[text] lines
evals.prompt: Create a Live Script called WrapDemo.m that introduces the Fourier transform. Start with a single multi-sentence introductory paragraph (about three sentences) that explains what the Fourier transform does and why it is useful. Then add one section showing a simple fft example on a small synthesized signal. Save to current directory.

assertions.description: Live Script file was created
The multi-sentence intro paragraph is on a single %[text] line
assertions.expected: The introductory paragraph (the one explaining the Fourier transform) appears on a SINGLE %[text] line containing all of its sentences. PASS: one continuous %[text] line with all ~3 sentences of the intro paragraph. FAIL: the intro paragraph is hard-wrapped across two or more consecutive %[text] lines. Note: a separate %[text] line for a different paragraph (e.g. a section blurb later in the file) is not a violation — only the intro paragraph is in scope.

assertions.description: The generated Live Script runs without error

evals.name: Table of Contents and External Hyperlink
evals.description: Tests that the skill uses the documented Table of Contents marker and external hyperlink syntax
evals.prompt: Create a Live Script called RefDemo.m about MATLAB plotting basics. Include a Table of Contents at the top (under the title). Have at least two sections so the TOC has entries. In the intro paragraph, include a link to the MATLAB documentation at https://www.mathworks.com/help/matlab/. Save to current directory.

assertions.description: Live Script file was created
Uses Table of Contents marker
External hyperlink to the MATLAB docs is present
The generated Live Script runs without error
